docs.title: Multilingual Multimodal Intelligent Application Dataset
docs.text:
This website presents a multilingual multimodal dataset designed to support intelligent applications, including speech recognition, speech translation, and multimodal understanding.

The dataset integrates video, audio, and multilingual text with precise time alignment, providing a solid foundation for data-driven research and model development.

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text:
• Multilingual speech and text data
  • Audio–video–text temporal alignment
  • Application-oriented dataset design
  • Suitable for speech and multimodal AI research

docs.title: Citation
docs.text:
If you use this dataset in your research, please cite it as follows:

```bibtex @dataset{scarlet2025mm_dataset, author = {Tian Xiangxiang,Zhu Liping}, title = {Multilingual Multimodal Intelligent Application Dataset}, year = {2025}, url = {https://github.com/Scarlet-xx/MmAZiad} }

docs.title: Dataset Composition
docs.text: The dataset consists of synchronized multimodal data segments extracted from real-world video sources.

docs.title: Modalities
docs.text:
• Video: speaker-centered clips
  • Audio: high-quality speech segments
  • Text: multilingual transcriptions and translations

docs.title: Segment Design
docs.text:
Each data segment is indexed by a unified timestamp and includes:

  • Video clip
  • Corresponding audio
  • Source-language transcript
  • Target-language translation

This design ensures seamless integration into downstream intelligent applications.

docs.title: Multimodal Alignment Demo
docs.text: This demo illustrates the temporal alignment between video, audio, and text.

This example demonstrates accurate synchronization across modalities.

docs.title: Data Statistics
docs.text: This section provides statistical visualizations to help understand the dataset distribution and identify potential biases.

docs.title: Summary
docs.text:
• Balanced multilingual distribution
  • Segment durations suitable for speech models
  • Consistent multimodal alignment across samples
